content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:codex/gpt-5.5-xhigh

## Summary

Two amendments identify real no-op/idempotency failure paths. The CLI task-id amendment is not supported by the current identity model in the code.

## Votes

- `fr-10-c43dddd4`: Accept
  The code treats an already-present amendment marker as a reason to call sync_own_dirty_if_beads_healthy even when it posted no comment. With a working-dir adapter, that can fail on unrelated or foreign pending br mutation state despite this reconciliation being a pure no-op. The needed retry behavior should be tied to this adapter owning recovered pending mutation state, not to seeing an existing marker alone.
- `fr-10-4816d9d7`: Accept
  reconcile_proposed_bead_records reads proposed-beads.ndjson before it knows whether any propose_new_bead group can produce a record. That makes fix_current_bead, informational_only, and below-threshold proposal paths fail on an unrelated file I/O problem even though they have no dedupe or write work to do. Filtering and thresholding first is safer and more maintainable.
- `fr-10-41a341a5`: Reject
  The duplicated project_id arguments are consistent with the CLI path, where project_id is used as the controller active_task_id and TaskSource does not carry a daemon task id. Daemon reconciliation paths pass the real daemon task.task_id separately. In this terminal-classification path, task_id is used for error/tracing context, while idempotency markers are based on amendment_id/run data, so the claimed marker correctness issue is not demonstrated.
